author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Shipped the snapshot cache at `crates/jjf-storage/src/cache.rs`.
On-disk file at `.jj/jjforge-cache.json`, atomic-write via
`.tmp` + rename, keyed by `head_commit` of the `issues`
bookmark. Cache rebuild reads everything in two batched
`jj file show` calls (one for `issues/`, one for `memories/`)
with a sentinel-separated `-T` template — rejected the design
doc's working-copy-walk because batched-show is faster AND
non-destructive (`@` stays where it is).

`Storage::list_ids`, `list_ready`, `list_memories`,
`read_memory`, `resolve`, `read`, `dep_tree`, and
`find_open_slug_collision` all consult the cache. Mutators
call `invalidate_snapshot_memo` after the 4-CLI dance so the
next read re-probes and rebuilds.

Per-Storage in-process memo (`Arc<Mutex<Option<Arc<...>>>>`)
shares the loaded cache across multiple read calls within
one CLI invocation. Same Storage clone shares the memo by
design.

Measured at `experiments/scale-index/` (Apple Silicon,
jj 0.40.0):

| N | list_ready cold/on-disk hit | warm memo | cold rebuild |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| 17 | 14ms | 0ms | 36ms |
| 100 | 14ms | 0ms | 42ms |
| 1000 | 15ms | 0ms | 84ms |
| 10000 | 23ms | 2ms | 554ms |

Pre-cache numbers (research doc): N=1000 was 22.5s, N=10000
extrapolated 225s. Steady-state win is ~1500x at N=1000,
~10000x at N=10000.

Source-tree smoke test: `jjf ready` against the real
jjforge planner is 65ms cold / 52ms warm vs. ~1.1s before.

# Recommendation

Ship as committed in `69f3043`. The design doc's working-copy-
walk rebuild is now obsolete in favor of the batched
`jj file show` approach (1-2 spawns total regardless of N,
no `@` mutation). Update the design doc as a follow-up if we
want it to reflect what landed.

Most of the win is from batching the `jj file show` calls
(one spawn replaces N×2). The on-disk cache layer adds the
"persist across CLI invocations" win on top (the cache hit
path skips even the batched read). Both are necessary for the
final headline number; isolated, the batched read alone gets
list_ready at N=1000 from 22.5s to ~80ms, and the cache layer
shaves that to 15ms.

# Confidence

High. 366 workspace tests pass (was 353; +8 cache integration
tests + 5 cache unit tests). Real-repo smoke test confirms
slugs resolve correctly post-write, ls/show match
post-mutation state, and cache file is gitignored by
`.jj/.gitignore`.

# Open follow-ups

- The design doc says "rebuild via working-copy walk"; what
  shipped is "rebuild via batched `jj file show`". Reword the
  doc.
- `read::read`'s debug cross-check no longer fires on cache
  hits (the cache is built from the same file projection).
  Cache rebuild doesn't run the cross-check either. If we want
  the invariant preserved, a follow-up could exercise it on
  cache miss.
- `cargo doc --doc` has a pre-existing doctest failure in
  `merge_ops.rs` line 19 — unrelated to this work but worth
  fixing.
